extraction.one_line_summary: KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren won from a reduced group sprint after BORA drove the peloton hard over climbs to shed BENNETT Sam before intermediate sprint, nearly succeeding in green jersey takeover.
tactical_patterns: BORA deployed entire team on Cat-4 climb before intermediate sprint specifically to drop BENNETT Sam and harvest points.
KÜNG Stefan's explosive TT acceleration bridged early gaps, showing time trial power translates to breakaway selection aggression.
BENNETT Sam's team fought hard on 6-mile climb to protect sprint points, revealing green jersey defense requires GC-level climbing domestiques.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: BORA orchestrated full team climb tempo before intermediate sprint, tactical sophistication shows desperation translating to coordinated attacks
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: struggled on climbs when BORA pressured before intermediate sprint, green jersey vulnerable on hilly sprint stages
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: positive: used TT power for violent acceleration to bridge breakaway gaps, time trial specialists underrated for breakaway formation
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: held intermediate sprint position despite early climb pressure, positioning discipline under duress
rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: positive: second stage win shows SUNWEB building momentum despite losing DUMOULIN Tom, team depth compensating GC loss
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: mechanical/positioning issue nearly cost time, curse continues with timing vulnerabilities
